rulings.Case Title: CAROL NICOLA VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 3, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Research-Cottrell, Inc.'s motion to strike plaintiff's complaint is denied. Even assuming that at the time she filed her complaint plaintiff was not ignorant of defendant's identity or facts giving rise to its liability, based on the evidence before the Court the statute of limitations has not run. (C.C.P. § 340.2.) Instead of filing a Doe amendment plaintiff could have filed an amended complaint naming Research-Cottrell, Inc. as a defendant. Therefore this Court treats plaintiff's Doe amendment the same way it would treat an amended complaint. (Davis v. Marin (2000) 80 Cal.App.4th 380.) Such an amended complaint would be proper because the statute of limitations has not run. Additionally, defendant did not demonstrate unreasonable delay that resulted in specific prejudice to defendant. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted in its entirety. While the motion is titled a "motion to strike," defendant does not move pursuant to C.C.P. sections 435-437. The Court focuses on the substance of the motion and considers it an evidence-based motion. (See A.N. v. County of Los Angeles (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1058; Barrows v. American Motors Corp. (1983) 144 Cal.App.3d 1.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/LOT)
